    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression\n",
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "\n",
        "# 1. Reload the dataset to ensure a clean slate\n",
        "df_house = pd.read_csv('/content/sample_data/california_housing_train.csv')\n",
        "\n",
        "# 2. Separate our features (X) from our target variable (y)\n",
        "X = df_house[['median_income', 'housing_median_age']]\n",
        "y = df_house['median_house_value']\n",
        "\n",
        "# 3. Initialize and train the Linear Regression AI model\n",
        "model = LinearRegression()\n",
        "model.fit(X, y)\n",
        "\n",
        "print(\"🤖 MACHINE LEARNING MODEL TRAINED SUCCESSFULLY!\")\n"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "O8XPX-TPbIqR",
        "outputId": "a6cb54d8-334e-4f21-d5e0-80bf517feb86"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "🤖 MACHINE LEARNING MODEL TRAINED SUCCESSFULLY!\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "\n",
        "# 1. Create a tiny DataFrame for our new neighborhood matching our exact feature names\n",
        "new_neighborhood = pd.DataFrame([[6.5, 15]], columns=['median_income', 'housing_median_age'])\n",
        "\n",
        "# 2. Use our trained model to predict the price\n",
        "predicted_price = model.predict(new_neighborhood)\n",
        "\n",
        "print(\"🔮 --- REAL ESTATE PRICE PREDICTION --- 🔮\")\n",
        "print(f\"Estimated Neighborhood Home Value: ${predicted_price[0]:,.2f}\")\n"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "JzQ3aWSvbTkC",
        "outputId": "95dc7bf3-2590-4f70-dac3-4c4d9f8f99f7"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "🔮 --- REAL ESTATE PRICE PREDICTION --- 🔮\n",
            "Estimated Neighborhood Home Value: $297,095.09\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "\n",
        "# Load data and take a small random sample of 200 neighborhoods so the mobile chart isn't crowded\n",
        "df_house = pd.read_csv('/content/sample_data/california_housing_train.csv')\n",
        "sample_df = df_house.sample(200, random_state=42)\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.figure(figsize=(9, 5))\n",
        "\n",
        "# Plot the scatter points\n",
        "plt.scatter(sample_df['median_income'], sample_df['median_house_value'], color='teal', alpha=0.7, edgecolors='black')\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.title('Real Estate Insights: Income Level vs. House Price')\n",
        "plt.xlabel('Neighborhood Median Income (in $10,000s)')\n",
        "plt.ylabel('Median House Value ($)')\n",
        "plt.grid(True, linestyle=':', alpha=0.6)\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.show()\n"
      ],
      "metadata": {
